sentiment.rationale: Designates Joseph Smith as prophet and seer with blessings promised to all who labor in the vineyard—affirming and comforting in tone.
Short, firm declaration that old covenants and prior baptisms are done away—authoritative correction requiring new ordinance.
Brief personal counsel to five individuals with encouragement, though Oliver is warned against pride.
Instructions to Joseph and Oliver include both comfort in affliction and stern warnings about cursings on those who reject them.
Tender revelation to Emma Smith as 'elect lady' with counsel on hymns, meekness, and the soul delighting in the song of the heart.
Brief, calm instruction to study scriptures and establish common consent—procedural and peaceful.
Grand vision of dispensation heads—Moroni, Elias, Elijah, Michael, Peter, James, John—gathered with Christ, climaxing in the armor of God.
Firm correction that only Joseph Smith receives commandments for the church; Hiram Page's seer stone is declared to be from Satan.
Sweeping prophecy of Second Coming destruction, final judgment, and the Fall—heavy in judgment language but balanced by the redemption of little children.
David Whitmer rebuked for fearing man; personal corrections to three individuals mixed with missionary assignments.
Warm personal revelation to Thomas B. Marsh blessing his family, calling him to preach, and promising the Lord's presence through afflictions.
Brief, encouraging call for Parley P. Pratt and Ziba Peterson to join the Lamanite mission with Christ as their advocate.
Urgent eleventh-hour harvest call to Ezra Thayre and Northrop Sweet, declaring the vineyard corrupted but the kingdom of heaven at hand.
Orson Pratt called with Second Coming imagery—Christ coming in clouds with power and great glory—emphasizing the imminence of His return.
Sidney Rigdon called as John-the-Baptist-like forerunner; promises Zion flourishing, Israel saved, and the kingdom given to the little flock.
Edward Partridge forgiven and called to preach; brief and encouraging with the promise of Christ's sudden coming to His temple.
Short, practical command to move to Ohio because of enemies—no grandeur, just protective urgency.
Christ declares omniscience, warns of secret enemies conspiring against the saints, commands gathering to Ohio, and insists on equality and caring for the poor.
James Covill called to be baptized and preach; recognized for past sorrow and pride, offered deliverance and a greater work.
Terse three-verse revelation that James Covill broke his covenant with God due to Satan's temptation and fear of persecution.

summaries.summary: The Lord designates Joseph Smith as a seer, translator, prophet, and apostle, commanding the church to receive his words as if from God's own mouth.
The Lord declares that all old covenants and prior baptisms are done away, requiring everyone to be baptized anew under the restored gospel.
Brief individual revelations to Oliver Cowdery, Hyrum Smith, Samuel Smith, Joseph Smith Sr., and Joseph Knight Sr. with personal counsel and callings.
The Lord outlines the duties of Joseph Smith and Oliver Cowdery as traveling ministers, including preaching, confirming churches, and traveling without purse or scrip.
The Lord calls Emma Smith an elect lady, counsels her to comfort Joseph, compile a hymn book, and delight in the song of the heart.
The Lord instructs the saints to study scriptures and establishes the principle that all things in the church shall be done by common consent.
The Lord teaches that the intent of the sacrament matters more than the substance, then names the ancient prophets and apostles who restored priesthood keys.
The Lord establishes that only Joseph Smith receives commandments for the church, corrects the Hiram Page seer stone incident, and sends Oliver to the Lamanites.
Christ reveals sweeping prophecies of the Second Coming, the Millennium, and final judgment, then teaches about the Fall, agency, and the innocence of little children.
Personal corrections and missionary assignments to David Whitmer, Peter Whitmer Jr., and John Whitmer, with David rebuked for fearing man over God.
The Lord calls Thomas B. Marsh to preach the gospel, blesses his family, and counsels patience in afflictions and meekness in governing his household.
The Lord calls Parley P. Pratt and Ziba Peterson to join Oliver Cowdery and Peter Whitmer Jr. on the mission to the Lamanites.
The Lord calls Ezra Thayre and Northrop Sweet to preach repentance in the eleventh hour, declaring the field white to harvest and the Bridegroom coming quickly.
The Lord calls young Orson Pratt to preach repentance and prepare the way for the Second Coming, promising to be with him until Christ returns.
The Lord calls Sidney Rigdon to write for Joseph Smith and preach the gospel, comparing him to John the Baptist in preparing the way for the Restoration.
The Lord forgives Edward Partridge and calls him to preach, commanding that all who embrace the calling be ordained and sent forth among the nations.
The Lord commands Joseph Smith and the church to stop translating and gather to Ohio for their safety against enemies.
Christ declares His omniscience, warns of secret enemies, promises a land of inheritance in Ohio with endowment of power, and commands equality and care for the poor.
The Lord calls James Covill, a longtime Baptist minister, to be baptized, receive the Holy Ghost, and preach the fulness of the gospel in Ohio.
The Lord reveals that James Covill broke his covenant because Satan tempted him with fear of persecution and the cares of the world.

crossReferences.note: Both teach that the prophet's words should be received as if from God's own mouth.
Emma called 'elect lady' echoes John's epistle addressing 'the elect lady and her children.'
Both celebrate sacred song as prayer and worship pleasing to God.
Christ directly invokes Paul's armor of God imagery—loins girt with truth, breastplate of righteousness, shield of faith.
Both describe Satan drawing a third part of heaven's hosts away through rebellion.
Both teach that little children are redeemed and cannot sin because they lack accountability.
Both describe Christ dwelling with the righteous on earth for a thousand years.
Both reference the city of Enoch (Zion) being taken into God's bosom.
Christ's command to 'be one' echoes His intercessory prayer that His followers may be one as He and the Father are one.
Both passages command baptism to 'wash away your sins, calling on my name.'
James Covill's story mirrors the Parable of the Sower—receiving the word with gladness but falling away due to persecution and worldly cares.

verses.reason: 'For his word ye shall receive, as if from mine own mouth'—foundational principle of prophetic authority in the LDS Church.
Declares all prior baptisms invalid under the new covenant—establishing the necessity of restored priesthood authority.
'Be patient in afflictions, for thou shalt have many; but endure them, for, lo, I am with thee, even unto the end of thy days.'
'For my soul delighteth in the song of the heart; yea, the song of the righteous is a prayer unto me'—foundational verse on sacred music in LDS worship.
Emma called 'an elect lady, whom I have called'—the origin of the Relief Society's scriptural identity.
'All things shall be done by common consent in the church'—the principle governing LDS church governance.
Names the dispensation heads—Moroni, Elias, Elijah, Michael, Peter, James, John—who restored priesthood keys.
'No one shall be appointed to receive commandments and revelations in this church excepting my servant Joseph Smith'—establishing the principle of a single presiding prophet.
'Little children are redeemed from the foundation of the world through mine Only Begotten'—key verse on the innocence and salvation of children.
'If they never should have bitter they could not know the sweet'—classic statement of the necessity of opposition.
Satan and a third of heaven's hosts cast out for rebellion—key verse on the pre-mortal war in heaven.
'Be patient in afflictions, revile not against those that revile. Govern your house in meekness, and be steadfast.'
'Having your lamps trimmed and burning, and oil with you, that you may be ready at the coming of the Bridegroom'—parable of the ten virgins applied.
'The time is soon at hand that I shall come in a cloud with power and great glory'—vivid Second Coming declaration.
'Fear not, little flock, the kingdom is yours until I come'—echoes Luke 12:32 with Restoration context.
'I call upon the weak things of the world, those who are unlearned and despised, to thrash the nations by the power of my Spirit.'
'I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God; wherefore, gird up your loins and I will suddenly come to my temple.'
'I say unto you, be one; and if ye are not one ye are not mine'—among the most quoted D&C verses on unity.
'If ye are prepared ye shall not fear'—widely quoted counsel on spiritual and temporal readiness.
Promise to be 'endowed with power from on high' in Ohio—foreshadowing the Kirtland Temple endowment.
Clear definition of the gospel: repentance, baptism by water, then the baptism of fire and the Holy Ghost.
'He received the word with gladness, but straightway Satan tempted him'—a compact lesson on the fragility of new conversion.
